title: Mailchimp vs ConvertKit (2025): Which Is Better?
meta_description: Compare Mailchimp and ConvertKit. See pricing, features, and which email marketing platform is best for your needs.
intro: Mailchimp and ConvertKit are both popular email marketing platforms, but they serve different audiences and use cases. Mailchimp positions itself as an all-in-one marketing solution for beginners and small businesses, while ConvertKit caters specifically to creators, bloggers, and course sellers. This comparison will help you determine which platform aligns best with your business goals.
choose_a_if: You're a beginner looking for the most affordable entry point into email marketing
You need a broad, all-in-one platform with landing pages, analytics, and A/B testing
You want to maximize your free tier with 500 subscribers before upgrading
choose_b_if: You're a creator, blogger, or course seller with specific audience needs
You need built-in commerce features to sell products directly to subscribers
You value customer support and community feedback (higher Trustpilot rating)

faq.a: It depends on your needs. Mailchimp is better for budget-conscious beginners seeking an affordable all-in-one platform, while ConvertKit excels for creators and course sellers who need specialized tools. ConvertKit has significantly higher customer satisfaction ratings, though it costs more.

faq.q: Which is cheaper, Mailchimp or ConvertKit?
faq.a: Mailchimp is considerably cheaper, starting at $13/month compared to ConvertKit's $29/month. Additionally, Mailchimp's free tier supports 500 subscribers versus ConvertKit's 1,000, making Mailchimp the more budget-friendly choice for price-sensitive users.

faq.q: Can I switch from ConvertKit to Mailchimp?
faq.a: Yes, switching between platforms is possible as both support subscriber list exports. Most users can export their contacts and import them into Mailchimp relatively easily. However, you may lose some automation sequences or custom tags during the transition, so plan accordingly.
